steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows a date within a relative clause. The next clause — 'Page's only model had been suspiciously destroyed' — is a complete independent clause. The blank must create a legal sentence boundary between the relative clause and that independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 1906—roughly six weeks earlier—Page's: a comma alone between an independent clause and another independent clause is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 1906. Roughly six weeks earlier, Page's: a semicolon here misplaces the punctuation mid-relative-clause, creating a fragment before it and a run-on after. ✗
  • Choice C — roughly six weeks earlier, Page's: the comma after 'earlier' still leaves a comma splice between the relative clause and the independent clause that follows. ✗
  • Choice D — 1906—roughly six weeks earlier. Page's: the em dashes set off 'roughly six weeks earlier' as a parenthetical, and the period creates a full sentence boundary so 'Page's only model had been…' begins a new, complete sentence. ✓
D is correct.
